AISuggestion.name: TimingErrors_Test5
AISuggestion.description: The CoffeeMachine behavior does not match the specification for drink preparation: after button selection, the model immediately reaches CoffeeDelivered or TeaDelivered without enforcing the required preparation times, and its timing attributes use ranges (coffee 5..10, tea 3..8) instead of the specified fixed durations of 10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that pressing the coffee button leads to a brewing state delayed exactly 10 seconds before CoffeeDelivered, and pressing the tea button leads to a brewing state delayed exactly 8 seconds before TeaDelivered. Replace the current ranged timing attributes with fixed delays matching the specification.

AISuggestion.name: TimingErrors_Test4
AISuggestion.description: The Wallet behavior does not match the specification: it currently waits between 50 and 70 seconds before inserting a coin, whereas the specification requires the wallet to take between 20 and 40 seconds to insert a coin.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Update the Wallet timing parameters so that the coin insertion delay is modeled with a minimum of 20 seconds and a maximum of 40 seconds, and keep the coin sending transition based on that corrected interval.

AISuggestion.name: TimingErrors_Test3
AISuggestion.description: The coffee machine behavior does not implement the required preparation durations for the selected beverage. According to the specification, coffee must take 10 seconds to brew and tea must take 8 seconds to prepare before the drink is delivered, but in the model the transitions after button selection trigger beverageReady immediately and reach CoffeeDelivered or TeaDelivered without any timing constraint.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that, after coffee selection, it enters a dedicated preparation state with a 10-second delay before CoffeeDelivered, and after tea selection, it enters a dedicated preparation state with an 8-second delay before TeaDelivered. If desired, the existing beverageReady action can be kept at the end of these timed preparation states.

AISuggestion.name: TimingErrors_Test2
AISuggestion.description: The CoffeeMachine behavior does not respect the specified timings: it waits 50 seconds instead of 30 seconds before ejecting coins when waiting for the second coin or for drink selection, and it does not model the required fixed preparation times of 10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea before delivery.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: In CoffeeMachine, change the timeout used in WaitingForSecondCoin and WaitingForSelection to 30 seconds, and introduce explicit preparation behavior after button selection so that coffee reaches its delivered state only after 10 seconds and tea only after 8 seconds.

AISuggestion.name: TimingErrors_Test1
AISuggestion.description: The Wallet behavior does not conform to the specification because the user coin insertion delay is modeled with `minUserDelay = 50` and `maxUserDelay = 70`, whereas the specification requires the wallet to take between 20 and 40 seconds to insert a coin.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Update the Wallet timing parameters so that the delay used before sending a coin is between 20 and 40 seconds, for example by setting `minUserDelay` to 20 and `maxUserDelay` to 40 in the Wallet block.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesign_Test5
AISuggestion.description: The model does not implement the tea dispensing scenario required by the specification. Although a tea button signal exists, the CoffeeMachine state machine never accepts pushTeaButton and never models tea preparation with its required 8-second duration. As a result, the system behavior only supports coffee, which is incomplete with respect to the specification.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Extend the CoffeeMachine behavior with a transition from the selection state that accepts pushTeaButton, then add a tea preparation/delivery state path with an 8-second delay before beverage completion. If desired structurally, also introduce an explicit TeaButton block or otherwise connect the existing tea button interaction so the tea request can actually be triggered in the model.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesign_Test4
AISuggestion.description: The coffee machine behavior is incomplete with respect to the specification: after two coins are inserted, the model only handles a coffee-button event and immediately reaches CoffeeDelivered, while the tea-button alternative is absent and no explicit preparation delay enforces 10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea before delivery.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Extend the CoffeeMachine state machine with two selection branches from WaitingForSelection, one triggered by pushCoffeeButton and one by pushTeaButton. Add explicit preparation states or timed transitions so coffee is delivered after 10 seconds and tea after 8 seconds, then return to the initial coin-accepting state after beverage collection.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesign_Test3
AISuggestion.description: The model does not implement the specified tea dispensing path: there is no effective tea button interaction and no 8-second tea preparation behavior after two coins are deposited. Only the coffee selection path is modeled.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a tea selection path to the CoffeeMachine behavior by accepting pushTeaButton in the selection state, then model a tea preparation state with a fixed 8-second delay before beverage delivery. If the interaction architecture is kept button-based, add a TeaButton block or another source for pushTeaButton so the event can actually be produced.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesign_Test2
AISuggestion.description: The model does not implement the tea selection path required by the specification. Although a tea button signal exists, there is no modeled TeaButton interaction or transition from the machine's selection state to prepare and deliver tea with the required 8-second preparation time.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a tea-selection path to the coffee machine by modeling a TeaButton or equivalent event source, connecting it to the machine, and adding a transition from the selection state to a tea preparation state with an 8-second delay before delivery. Keep the existing coffee path distinct with its own 10-second preparation behavior.

AISuggestion.name: IncompleteDesign_Test1
AISuggestion.description: The specification requires the machine to prepare either tea or coffee when the corresponding button is pressed before coin ejection, with tea taking 8 seconds and coffee 10 seconds. In the model, CoffeeMachine only handles pushCoffeeButton in WaitingForSelection, triggers beverageReady immediately, and has no tea preparation path or tea-specific timing.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Extend CoffeeMachine with a transition from WaitingForSelection that accepts pushTeaButton, add a tea preparation state with an 8-second delay, and make the coffee path explicit with a 10-second delay before delivery. If needed, add a TeaButton block or another sender connected to pushTeaButton.

TURTLEGMODELING.SPECIFICATION:
This coffee machine dispenses a beverage only after two coins have been deposited. If there's a substantial delay between (i.e., more than 30 seconds) the insertion of the first and second coins, the machine returns the initial coin. Likewise, if a beverage isn't selected promptly after the deposit of the two coins, both coins are automatically ejected. If either of the beverage buttons (tea or coffee) is pressed before the coins are ejected, the machine begins to prepare the selected drink. Notably, it takes 10 seconds to brew a coffee and 8 seconds to make a tea. Once the beverage has been collected, the machine is ready to accept new coins for the next order.
The user is represented as a Wallet that contains two coins when the system starts. The wallet takes between 20 and 40 seconds to insert a coin.
Use between 3 and 6 blocks.

textline.data: The availability of the machine shall be
A wallet must never contain a
The coffee machine never has one coin
inserted more than 50 units of time
When the application starts, if the wallet contains
at least 2 coins, then a beverage may be delivered.
On the contrary, if the wallet contains less than 2
coins, no beverage will be delivered by the

infoparam.name: TGComponent
infoparam.value: nbOfCoins >= 0

infoparam.name: TGComponent
infoparam.value: nbOfCoins > 1

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value: CoffeeMachine featuring two main functions: - coin ejection - tea or coffee selection

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value:
A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2
A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1
F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin
CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion Computation time: 165 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 6
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10 Model improvement: 9 Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification, yet one transition deletion in CoffeeMachine and one transition modification in TeaButton are needed to acheive full semantic accuracy
Mutations: add block TeaButton attach TeaButton to CoffeeMachine add attribute int mechanicalDelay = 10 in TeaButton add output signal push in TeaButton add state main in TeaButton add transition in TeaButton from start to main add transition in TeaButton from main to main with after(10,10) and "push()" add state PreparingCoffee in CoffeeMachine add state PreparingTea in CoffeeMachine add state TeaDelivered in CoffeeMachine add action on signal ReceiveCoffeeSelection in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton() add action on signal ReceiveTeaSelection in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton() add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to ReceiveCoffeeSelection add transition in CoffeeMachine from ReceiveCoffeeSelection to PreparingCoffee add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingCoffee to CoffeeDelivered with after(10,10) add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to ReceiveTeaSelection add transition in CoffeeMachine from ReceiveTeaSelection to PreparingTea add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingTea to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin add connection from push in TeaButton to pushTeaButton in CoffeeMachine in private synchronous link
Description: The specification requires the machine to prepare either tea or coffee when the corresponding button is pressed before coin ejection, with tea taking 8 seconds and coffee 10 seconds. In the model, CoffeeMachine only handles pushCoffeeButton in WaitingForSelection, triggers beverageReady immediately, and has no tea preparation path or tea-specific timing.
How to solve: Extend CoffeeMachine with a transition from WaitingForSelection that accepts pushTeaButton, add a tea preparation state with an 8-second delay, and make the coffee path explicit with a 10-second delay before delivery. If needed, add a TeaButton block or another sender connected to pushTeaButton.

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10
Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification,
yet one transition deletion in CoffeeMachine and one transition modification
in TeaButton are needed to acheive full semantic accuracy
add attribute int mechanicalDelay = 10 in TeaButton
add output signal push in TeaButton
add transition in TeaButton from start to main
add transition in TeaButton from main to main with after(10,10) and "push()"
add action on signal ReceiveCoffeeSelection in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton()
add action on signal ReceiveTeaSelection in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton()
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to ReceiveCoffeeSelection
add transition in CoffeeMachine from ReceiveCoffeeSelection to PreparingCoffee
add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingCoffee to CoffeeDelivered with after(10,10)
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to ReceiveTeaSelection
add transition in CoffeeMachine from ReceiveTeaSelection to PreparingTea
add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingTea to TeaDelivered with after(8,8)
add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin
add connection from push in TeaButton to pushTeaButton in CoffeeMachine in private synchronous link
The specification requires the machine to prepare either tea or coffee when the corresponding button is pressed before coin ejection,

with tea taking 8 seconds and coffee 10 seconds. In the model, CoffeeMachine only handles pushCoffeeButton in WaitingForSelection, triggers beverageReady immediately, and has no tea preparation path or tea-specific timing.

Extend CoffeeMachine with a transition from WaitingForSelection that accepts pushTeaButton, add a tea preparation state with an 8-second delay, and make the coffee path explicit with a 10-second delay before delivery.
If needed, add a TeaButton block or another sender connected to pushTeaButton.

UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion Computation time: 154 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 7
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10 Model improvement: 95 Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification, yet one transition needs to be removed in CoffeeMachine to achive full semantic accuracy
Mutations: add block TeaButton attach TeaButton to CoffeeMachine add attribute int mechanicalDelay = 10 in TeaButton add output signal push in TeaButton add state main in TeaButton add transition in TeaButton from start to main add state pressed in TeaButton add action on signal sendPush in TeaButton with push() add transition in TeaButton from main to sendPush add transition in TeaButton from sendPush to pressed add transition in TeaButton from pressed to main with after(10,10) add connection from push in TeaButton to pushTeaButton in CoffeeMachine in private synchronous link add state TeaPreparation in CoffeeMachine add state TeaDelivered in CoffeeMachine add action on signal teaSelection in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton() add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to teaSelection add transition in CoffeeMachine from teaSelection to TeaPreparation with "nbOfCoins=0" add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaPreparation to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()" add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin add state CoffeePreparation in CoffeeMachine add action on signal coffeeSelection in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton() add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to coffeeSelection add transition in CoffeeMachine from coffeeSelection to CoffeePreparation with "nbOfCoins=0" add transition in CoffeeMachine from CoffeePreparation to CoffeeDelivered with after(10,10) and "beverageReady()"
Description: The model does not implement the tea selection path required by the specification. Although a tea button signal exists, there is no modeled TeaButton interaction or transition from the machine's selection state to prepare and deliver tea with the required 8-second preparation time.
How to solve: Add a tea-selection path to the coffee machine by modeling a TeaButton or equivalent event source, connecting it to the machine, and adding a transition from the selection state to a tea preparation state with an 8-second delay before delivery. Keep the existing coffee path distinct with its own 10-second preparation behavior.

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10
Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification, yet one transition needs to be removed in CoffeeMachine to achive full semantic accuracy
add attribute int mechanicalDelay = 10 in TeaButton
add output signal push in TeaButton
add transition in TeaButton from start to main
add action on signal sendPush in TeaButton with push()
add transition in TeaButton from main to sendPush
add transition in TeaButton from sendPush to pressed
add transition in TeaButton from pressed to main with after(10,10)
add connection from push in TeaButton to pushTeaButton in CoffeeMachine in private synchronous link
add action on signal teaSelection in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton()
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to teaSelection
add transition in CoffeeMachine from teaSelection to TeaPreparation with "nbOfCoins=0"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaPreparation to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin
add action on signal coffeeSelection in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton()
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to coffeeSelection
add transition in CoffeeMachine from coffeeSelection to CoffeePreparation with "nbOfCoins=0"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from CoffeePreparation to CoffeeDelivered with after(10,10) and "beverageReady()"
The model does not implement the tea selection path required by the specification. Although a tea button signal exists,
there is no modeled TeaButton interaction or transition from the machine's selection state to prepare and deliver tea with the required 8-second preparation time.
Add a tea-selection path to the coffee machine by modeling a TeaButton or equivalent event source, connecting it to the machine, and adding a transition from the selection
state to a tea preparation state with an 8-second delay before delivery. Keep the existing coffee path distinct with its own 10-second preparation behavior.

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value: A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1 F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion Computation time: 169 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 6
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10 Model improvement: 9 Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification yet two transitions need to be modified in CoffeeMachine and in TeaButton to achive full semantic accuracy
Mutations: add block TeaButton add output signal push in TeaButton add state main in TeaButton add transition in TeaButton from start to main add state state0 in TeaButton add state afterSending__0 in TeaButton add transition in TeaButton from main to state0 add transition in TeaButton from state0 to afterSending__0 with "push()" add transition in TeaButton from afterSending__0 to main with after(10,10) attach TeaButton to CoffeeMachine add connection from push in TeaButton to pushTeaButton in CoffeeMachine in private synchronous link add state TeaPreparation in CoffeeMachine add state TeaDelivered in CoffeeMachine add action on signal receiveTea in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton() add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to receiveTea add transition in CoffeeMachine from receiveTea to TeaPreparation with "nbOfCoins=0" add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaPreparation to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()" add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin
Description: The model does not implement the specified tea dispensing path: there is no effective tea button interaction and no 8-second tea preparation behavior after two coins are deposited. Only the coffee selection path is modeled.
How to solve: Add a tea selection path to the CoffeeMachine behavior by accepting pushTeaButton in the selection state, then model a tea preparation state with a fixed 8-second delay before beverage delivery. If the interaction architecture is kept button-based, add a TeaButton block or another source for pushTeaButton so the event can actually be produced.

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10
Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification
yet two transitions need to be modified in CoffeeMachine and in TeaButton to achive full semantic accuracy
add output signal push in TeaButton
add transition in TeaButton from start to main
add transition in TeaButton from main to state0
add transition in TeaButton from state0 to afterSending__0 with "push()"
add transition in TeaButton from afterSending__0 to main with after(10,10)
add connection from push in TeaButton to pushTeaButton in CoffeeMachine in private synchronous link
add action on signal receiveTea in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton()
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to receiveTea
add transition in CoffeeMachine from receiveTea to TeaPreparation with "nbOfCoins=0"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaPreparation to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin
The model does not implement the specified tea dispensing path: there is no effective tea button interaction and no 8-second
tea preparation behavior after two coins are deposited. Only the coffee selection path is modeled.
Add a tea selection path to the CoffeeMachine behavior by accepting pushTeaButton in the selection state, then model a tea

preparation state with a fixed 8-second delay before beverage delivery. If the interaction architecture is kept button-based, add a TeaButton block or another source for pushTeaButton so the event can actually be produced.

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value: A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1 F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Executable* suggestion Computation time: 244 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 7
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 7.5 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10 Model improvement: 5 Comments: The suggestion improves semantically the model: the CoffeeMachine's SMD has been enriched with timing values and a behavior for tea preparation. Yet, the model is not well-formed since no TeaButton block has been added to connect the signal pushTeaButton
Mutations: add state PreparingCoffee in CoffeeMachine add state PreparingTea in CoffeeMachine add state TeaDelivered in CoffeeMachine add action on signal receiveCoffee in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton() add action on signal receiveTea in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton() add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to receiveCoffee add transition in CoffeeMachine from receiveCoffee to PreparingCoffee with "nbOfCoins = 0" add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingCoffee to CoffeeDelivered with after(10,10) and "beverageReady()" add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to receiveTea add transition in CoffeeMachine from receiveTea to PreparingTea with "nbOfCoins = 0" add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingTea to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()" add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin
Description: The coffee machine behavior is incomplete with respect to the specification: after two coins are inserted, the model only handles a coffee-button event and immediately reaches CoffeeDelivered, while the tea-button alternative is absent and no explicit preparation delay enforces 10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea before delivery.
How to solve: Extend the CoffeeMachine state machine with two selection branches from WaitingForSelection, one triggered by pushCoffeeButton and one by pushTeaButton. Add explicit preparation states or timed transitions so coffee is delivered after 10 seconds and tea after 8 seconds, then return to the initial coin-accepting state after beverage collection.

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10
Comments: The suggestion improves semantically the model: the CoffeeMachine's SMD
has been enriched with timing values and a behavior for tea preparation. Yet, the model
is not well-formed since no TeaButton block has been added to connect the signal pushTeaButton
add action on signal receiveCoffee in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton()
add action on signal receiveTea in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton()
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to receiveCoffee
add transition in CoffeeMachine from receiveCoffee to PreparingCoffee with "nbOfCoins = 0"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingCoffee to CoffeeDelivered with after(10,10) and "beverageReady()"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to receiveTea
add transition in CoffeeMachine from receiveTea to PreparingTea with "nbOfCoins = 0"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingTea to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin
The coffee machine behavior is incomplete with respect to the specification: after two coins are inserted, the model only handles

a coffee-button event and immediately reaches CoffeeDelivered, while the tea-button alternative is absent and no explicit preparation delay enforces 10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea before delivery.

Extend the CoffeeMachine state machine with two selection branches from WaitingForSelection, one triggered by pushCoffeeButton and one by pushTeaButton. Add explicit preparation states or timed transitions
so coffee is delivered after 10 seconds and tea after 8 seconds, then return to the initial coin-accepting state after beverage collection.

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value: A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1 F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion Computation time: 116 s Semantic oracle evaluation: 9
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10 Model improvement: 9.5 Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification yet one transition need to be modified in CoffeeMachine to achive full semantic accuracy
Mutations: add block TeaButton attach TeaButton to CoffeeMachine add output signal push in TeaButton add state main in TeaButton add transition in TeaButton from start to main add state state0 in TeaButton add transition in TeaButton from main to state0 add action on signal sendPush in TeaButton with push() add transition in TeaButton from state0 to sendPush add state afterSending in TeaButton add transition in TeaButton from sendPush to afterSending add transition in TeaButton from afterSending to main with after(10,10) add private synchronous link between TeaButton and CoffeeMachine add connection from push in TeaButton to pushTeaButton in CoffeeMachine in private synchronous link add state TeaDelivered in CoffeeMachine add action on signal receiveTea in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton() add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to receiveTea add state TeaSelected in CoffeeMachine add transition in CoffeeMachine from receiveTea to TeaSelected add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaSelected to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()" add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin with "nbOfCoins=0"
Description: The model does not implement the tea dispensing scenario required by the specification. Although a tea button signal exists, the CoffeeMachine state machine never accepts pushTeaButton and never models tea preparation with its required 8-second duration. As a result, the system behavior only supports coffee, which is incomplete with respect to the specification.
How to solve: Extend the CoffeeMachine behavior with a transition from the selection state that accepts pushTeaButton, then add a tea preparation/delivery state path with an 8-second delay before beverage completion. If desired structurally, also introduce an explicit TeaButton block or otherwise connect the existing tea button interaction so the tea request can actually be triggered in the model.

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10
Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification
yet one transition need to be modified in CoffeeMachine to achive full semantic accuracy
add output signal push in TeaButton
add transition in TeaButton from start to main
add transition in TeaButton from main to state0
add action on signal sendPush in TeaButton with push()
add transition in TeaButton from state0 to sendPush
add transition in TeaButton from sendPush to afterSending
add transition in TeaButton from afterSending to main with after(10,10)
add private synchronous link between TeaButton and CoffeeMachine
add connection from push in TeaButton to pushTeaButton in CoffeeMachine in private synchronous link
add action on signal receiveTea in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton()
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to receiveTea
add transition in CoffeeMachine from receiveTea to TeaSelected
add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaSelected to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from TeaDelivered to WaitingForFirstCoin with "nbOfCoins=0"
The model does not implement the tea dispensing scenario required by the specification. Although a tea button

signal exists, the CoffeeMachine state machine never accepts pushTeaButton and never models tea preparation with its required 8-second duration. As a result, the system behavior only supports coffee, which is incomplete with respect to the specification.

Extend the CoffeeMachine behavior with a transition from the selection state that accepts pushTeaButton, then add a tea preparation/delivery state path with an 8-second delay before beverage completion. If desired structurally, also introduce an explicit
TeaButton block or otherwise connect the existing tea button interaction so the tea request can actually be triggered in the model.

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value: A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1 F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable suggestion* Computation time: 118s Semantic oracle evaluation: 6
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10 Model improvement: 10 Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification + no regression introduced
Mutations: remove attribute minUserDelay in Wallet add attribute int minUserDelay = 20 in Wallet remove attribute maxUserDelay in Wallet add attribute int maxUserDelay = 40 in Wallet
Description: The Wallet behavior does not conform to the specification because the user coin insertion delay is modeled with `minUserDelay = 50` and `maxUserDelay = 70`, whereas the specification requires the wallet to take between 20 and 40 seconds to insert a coin.
How to change: Update the Wallet timing parameters so that the delay used before sending a coin is between 20 and 40 seconds, for example by setting `minUserDelay` to 20 and `maxUserDelay` to 40 in the Wallet block.

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10
Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification + no regression introduced
add attribute int minUserDelay = 20 in Wallet
add attribute int maxUserDelay = 40 in Wallet
The Wallet behavior does not conform to the specification
because the user coin insertion delay is modeled with `minUserDelay = 50` and `maxUserDelay = 70`, whereas the specification requires the wallet to take between 20 and 40 seconds to insert a coin.

Update the Wallet timing parameters so that the delay used before sending a coin is between 20 and 40 seconds, for example by setting `minUserDelay` to 20 and `maxUserDelay` to 40 in the Wallet block.

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value: A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1 F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

infoparam.name: List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition

List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition
List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition
UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable suggestion* Computation time: 175s Semantic oracle evaluation: 9
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10 Model improvement: 10 Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification + no regression introduced. The error on the delays on the transitions is correctly identified: the initial CoffeeMachine's SMD features "computeFor" actions on transitions between waitingSelection and coffeeDelivered/teaDelivered, however, those actions are not exported to the SysML v2 format provided to the LLM, and are not taken into account in the TTool simulator. (see comment in TimingErrors_Test5 tab, which contains the exported SysML v2 model)
Mutations: remove attribute coinDelay in CoffeeMachine add attribute int coinDelay = 30 in CoffeeMachine add state PreparingCoffee in CoffeeMachine add state PreparingTea in CoffeeMachine remove action on signal in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton() remove action on signal in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton() add action on signal SelectCoffee in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton() add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to SelectCoffee add transition in CoffeeMachine from SelectCoffee to PreparingCoffee with "nbOfCoins=0" add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingCoffee to CoffeeDelivered with after(10,10) and "beverageReady()" add action on signal SelectTea in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton() add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to SelectTea add transition in CoffeeMachine from SelectTea to PreparingTea with "nbOfCoins=0" add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingTea to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()"
Description: The CoffeeMachine behavior does not respect the specified timings: it waits 50 seconds instead of 30 seconds before ejecting coins when waiting for the second coin or for drink selection, and it does not model the required fixed preparation times of 10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea before delivery.
How to change: In CoffeeMachine, change the timeout used in WaitingForSecondCoin and WaitingForSelection to 30 seconds, and introduce explicit preparation behavior after button selection so that coffee reaches its delivered state only after 10 seconds and tea only after 8 seconds.

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10
Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification + no regression introduced.
The error on the delays on the transitions is correctly identified: the initial CoffeeMachine's SMD features
"computeFor" actions on transitions between waitingSelection and coffeeDelivered/teaDelivered, however,
those actions are not exported to the SysML v2 format provided to the LLM, and are not taken into account
(see comment in TimingErrors_Test5 tab, which contains the exported SysML v2 model)
add attribute int coinDelay = 30 in CoffeeMachine
remove action on signal in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton()
remove action on signal in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton()
add action on signal SelectCoffee in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton()
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to SelectCoffee
add transition in CoffeeMachine from SelectCoffee to PreparingCoffee with "nbOfCoins=0"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingCoffee to CoffeeDelivered with after(10,10) and "beverageReady()"
add action on signal SelectTea in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton()
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to SelectTea
add transition in CoffeeMachine from SelectTea to PreparingTea with "nbOfCoins=0"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from PreparingTea to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()"
The CoffeeMachine behavior does not respect the specified timings: it waits 50 seconds instead of 30 seconds before ejecting coins
when waiting for the second coin or for drink selection, and it does not model the required fixed preparation times of 10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea before delivery.
In CoffeeMachine, change the timeout used in WaitingForSecondCoin and WaitingForSelection to 30 seconds, and introduce explicit preparation behavior after button selection so
that coffee reaches its delivered state only after 10 seconds and tea only after 8 seconds.

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value: A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1 F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Suggestion* Computation time: 255 s Semantic oracle evaluation (of the SysML v2 fallback): 10
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): N/A Model improvement: 0
Description: The coffee machine behavior does not implement the required preparation durations for the selected beverage. According to the specification, coffee must take 10 seconds to brew and tea must take 8 seconds to prepare before the drink is delivered, but in the model the transitions after button selection trigger beverageReady immediately and reach CoffeeDelivered or TeaDelivered without any timing constraint.
How to solve: Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that, after coffee selection, it enters a dedicated preparation state with a 10-second delay before CoffeeDelivered, and after tea selection, it enters a dedicated preparation state with an 8-second delay before TeaDelivered. If desired, the existing beverageReady action can be kept at the end of these timed preparation states.

Line.value: Semantic oracle evaluation (of the SysML v2 fallback): 10
Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): N/A
The coffee machine behavior does not implement the required preparation durations for the selected beverage.

According to the specification, coffee must take 10 seconds to brew and tea must take 8 seconds to prepare before the drink is delivered, but in the model the transitions after button selection trigger beverageReady immediately and reach CoffeeDelivered or TeaDelivered without any timing constraint.

Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that, after coffee selection, it enters a dedicated preparation state with a 10-second delay before CoffeeDelivered, and after tea selection, it enters a dedicated preparation state with an 8-second delay before TeaDelivered. If desired, the existing beverageReady
action can be kept at the end of these timed preparation states.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value: CoffeeMachine featuring two main functions: - coin ejection - tea or coffee selection

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value:
A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2
A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1
F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin
CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

connector from state0 to Send signal

connector from start state to state0
List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition
UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable suggestion* Computation time: 115s Semantic oracle evaluation: 7
Our evaluation: Suggestion relevance: 10 Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10 Model improvement: 10 Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification + no regression introduced
Mutations: mutations: remove attribute minUserDelay in Wallet add attribute int minUserDelay = 20 in Wallet remove attribute maxUserDelay in Wallet add attribute int maxUserDelay = 40 in Wallet
Description: The Wallet behavior does not match the specification: it currently waits between 50 and 70 seconds before inserting a coin, whereas the specification requires the wallet to take between 20 and 40 seconds to insert a coin.
How to change: Update the Wallet timing parameters so that the coin insertion delay is modeled with a minimum of 20 seconds and a maximum of 40 seconds, and keep the coin sending transition based on that corrected interval.

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10
Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification + no regression introduced
mutations: remove attribute minUserDelay in Wallet
add attribute int minUserDelay = 20 in Wallet
add attribute int maxUserDelay = 40 in Wallet
The Wallet behavior does not match the specification: it
currently waits between 50 and 70 seconds before inserting a coin, whereas the specification requires the wallet to take between 20 and 40 seconds to insert a coin.
Update the Wallet timing parameters so that the coin insertion delay is modeled with a minimum of 20 seconds and a maximum of 40 seconds, and keep the coin sending
transition based on that corrected interval.

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value: A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1 F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3

infoparam.name: List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition

List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition
List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition
List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition
List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition
List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition
List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition
List of all parameters of an Avatar SMD transition
UML Note

Line.value: Mutation script accuracy wrt to the suggestion (how to solve): 10
Comments: suggestion fully adressed + higher model quality wrt specification + no regression introduced.
The error on the delays on the transitions is correctly identified: the initial CoffeeMachine's SMD features
"computeFor" actions on transitions between waitingSelection and coffeeDelivered/teaDelivered, however,
those actions are not exported to the SysML v2 format provided to the LLM, and are not taken into account
add attribute int teaDelay = 8 in CoffeeMachine
add attribute int coffeeDelay = 10 in CoffeeMachine
remove action on signal in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton()
remove action on signal in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton()
add action on signal SelectCoffee in CoffeeMachine with pushCoffeeButton()
add action on signal SelectTea in CoffeeMachine with pushTeaButton()
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to SelectCoffee
add transition in CoffeeMachine from SelectCoffee to BrewingCoffee with "nbOfCoins = 0"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from BrewingCoffee to CoffeeDelivered with after(10,10) and "beverageReady()"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from WaitingForSelection to SelectTea
add transition in CoffeeMachine from SelectTea to BrewingTea with "nbOfCoins = 0"
add transition in CoffeeMachine from BrewingTea to TeaDelivered with after(8,8) and "beverageReady()"
The CoffeeMachine behavior does not match the specification for drink preparation: after button selection, the model

immediately reaches CoffeeDelivered or TeaDelivered without enforcing the required preparation times, and its timing attributes use ranges (coffee 5..10, tea 3..8) instead of the specified fixed durations of 10 seconds for coffee and 8 seconds for tea.

Modify the CoffeeMachine state machine so that pressing the coffee button leads to a brewing state delayed exactly 10 seconds before CoffeeDelivered, and pressing the tea button leads to a brewing state
delayed exactly 8 seconds before TeaDelivered. Replace the current ranged timing attributes with fixed delays matching the specification.

infoparam.name: UPPAAL Pragma
infoparam.value: A[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<=2 T E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>0 E<> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins>1 E[] CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins<3 A[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=0 E[] Wallet.nbOfCoins>=1 F CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered && CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered || CoffeeMachine.TeaDelivered CoffeeMachine.Beverage --> CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin F CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState CoffeeMachine.WaitingForFirstCoin --> CoffeeMachine.EjectState || CoffeeMachine.WaitingForSecondCoin CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<2 CoffeeMachine.CoffeeDelivered --> CoffeeMachine.nbOfCoins<3
